name: Insulin WITHOUT Diabetes Diagnosis (Negation / Undocumented or Steroid-Induced)
description: **Negation test for clinically-meaningful undocumented disease.** Find patients prescribed insulin who do NOT have a documented type 1 OR type 2 diabetes diagnosis. Real-world causes: (1) newly insulin-requiring T1D before formal diagnosis, (2) steroid-induced hyperglycemia in chronic-steroid patients, (3) gestational diabetes (resolved), (4) charting gaps. This is one of the most clinically actionable negation queries — these patients should be flagged for endocrinology review. Tests two-query subtraction across BOTH T1D and T2D diagnosis codes.
prompt: Find patients prescribed insulin (any form — regular, glargine, aspart, etc.) who do NOT have a documented diagnosis of type 1 diabetes OR type 2 diabetes. These represent clinically important undocumented populations: undiagnosed diabetes, steroid-induced hyperglycemia, or charting gaps that should trigger endocrinology referral.

prompts.broad: Find patients with insulin prescriptions (any insulin product) but NO type 1 diabetes diagnosis AND NO type 2 diabetes diagnosis. Use a two-query subtraction approach: insulin patients minus diabetes-diagnosed patients (both T1D and T2D codes combined).
prompts.expert: Set difference: MedicationRequest patients on any insulin MINUS Condition patients with T1D OR T2D diagnoses. Two queries: (1) `MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5856,274783,311036,253182,575068` (insulin variants), (2) `Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|46635009,199229001,23045005,31321000119102,190368000,44054006&code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11` (T1D + T2D codes combined). Return query 1 MINUS query 2. The diagnosis subtraction must include BOTH T1D and T2D codes — neither alone is sufficient.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Set difference (insulin patients) MINUS (T1D OR T2D-diagnosed patients). Tests negation with multi-disease union on the subtracted side.
